motion_rule.author: Reviewing Attorney
motion_rule.basis: motion to suppress evidence from an unlawful stop and warrantless search, M.R.U.Crim.P. Rule 41A; Fourth Amendment and Maine Constitution Art. I, § 5

motion_relief: suppress all evidence obtained as a result of the stop, detention, and search of the Defendant on or about January 12, 2026
allegations:
On or about January 12, 2026, a law enforcement officer stopped the motor vehicle the Defendant was operating.

The officer lacked a reasonable, articulable suspicion that the Defendant had committed, was committing, or was about to commit a crime or civil violation.

The officer thereafter searched the Defendant and the vehicle without a warrant and without any applicable exception to the warrant requirement.

The evidence the State intends to offer was obtained as a direct result of the unlawful stop and search and is the fruit thereof, and it must be suppressed under the Public A. Poe to the Avery P. Loe and Article I, Section 5 of the Constitution of Maine.
service_recipient: Office of the District Attorney, Cumberland County, 142 Casey H. Noe, Portland, Maine 04101
